content.content:
1	{
2	  "body": "Based on discussions with @hattrill and @ValWood , and on comments from @raymond91125 in https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30552\n\nit was proposed to update the definitions of protein carrier activity and unfolded protein holdase activity as follows:\n\n- [x] protein carrier activity GO:0140597: \nCurrent definition: Binding to and carrying a protein between two different locations by moving along with the target protein.\nNew definition: Binding to and carrying a protein to an acceptor molecule or to a specific location by moving along with the target protein.\n\n- [x] unfolded protein holdase activity GO:0140309\nCurrent definition: A protein carrier activity that binds to a protein in an unfolded state and escorts it between two different locations. The unfolded protein carrier prevents aggregation of the target protein while it's being delivers to its final destination.\nNew definition: A protein carrier activity that binds to a protein in an unfolded state and escorts it to an acceptor molecule or to a specific location. The unfolded protein carrier prevents aggregation of the target protein while it's being delivers to its final destination.",
3	  "comments": [
4	    {
5	      "id": "IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABAoQMZw",
6	      "author": {
7	        "login": "hattrill"
8	      },
9	      "authorAssociation": "MEMBER",
10	      "body": "Sorry to reopen this (then accidently close it then reopen it).\nThe def for 'protein carrier activity' GO:0140597 is still too restrictive, could we make it more similar to the parent term i.e.\n\"Directly binding to a protein and delivering it either to an acceptor molecule or to a specific location.\"",
11	      "createdAt": "2026-04-28T16:26:23Z",
12	      "includesCreatedEdit": false,
13	      "isMinimized": false,
14	      "minimizedReason": "",

18	    },

23	      },

25	      "body": "@dragon-ai-agent please revise GO:0140597 definition as commented by hattrill.",

input.description: Look up GO:0140309 (unfolded protein holdase activity)

content.content:
[Term]
id: GO:0140309
name: unfolded protein holdase activity
namespace: molecular_function
def: "A protein carrier activity that binds to a protein in an unfolded state and escorts it to an acceptor molecule or to a specific location. The unfolded protein carrier prevents aggregation of the target protein while it's being delivers to its final destination." [PMID:39488384]
comment: Note that an holdase binds an unfolded protein and keeps it unfolded, unlike a protein folding chaperone, which binds an unfolded protein to fold it.
synonym: "holdase" EXACT []
synonym: "holdase-carrier chaperone" EXACT []
synonym: "unfolded protein carrier activity" EXACT []
is_a: GO:0140597 ! protein carrier activity
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30552" xsd:anyURI
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31601" xsd:anyURI

content.content:
[Term]
id: GO:0140104
name: molecular carrier activity
namespace: molecular_function
def: "Directly binding to a specific ion or molecule and delivering it either to an acceptor molecule or to a specific location." [GOC:molecular_function_refactoring, GOC:pdt]
comment: Note that a carrier moves with its substrate/cargo, while a transporter does not move with the cargo, but facilitates the change in localization.

input.command:
git add src/ontology/go-edit.obo && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Update definition of GO:0140597 (protein carrier activity)

Per request in issue #31601, revise the textual definition of
protein carrier activity (GO:0140597) to align more closely with
the parent term GO:0140104 (molecular carrier activity).

Old: "Binding to and carrying a protein to an acceptor molecule or
to a specific location by moving along with the target protein."

New: "Directly binding to a protein and delivering it either to an
acceptor molecule or to a specific location."

The previous definition was felt to be too restrictive; the new
wording mirrors the parent definition structure (which begins with
"Directly binding to a specific ion or molecule and delivering it
either to an acceptor molecule or to a specific location."),
replacing the cargo with "protein".

GO:0140309 (unfolded protein holdase activity) was already updated
to the agreed wording in a prior change and required no edit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.content:
## Summary

Updates the textual definition of `protein carrier activity` (GO:0140597) to align with the parent term `molecular carrier activity` (GO:0140104), per @hattrill's request in geneontology/go-ontology#31601 and follow-up assignment from @raymond91125.

### Change

`GO:0140597` def
- Old: `Binding to and carrying a protein to an acceptor molecule or to a specific location by moving along with the target protein.`
- New: `Directly binding to a protein and delivering it either to an acceptor molecule or to a specific location.`

The new wording mirrors the parent definition for `GO:0140104 molecular carrier activity` (`Directly binding to a specific ion or molecule and delivering it either to an acceptor molecule or to a specific location.`), substituting the cargo. The "moves with the cargo" semantics that distinguish a carrier from a transporter remain captured in the parent's comment, and `GO:0140597`'s logical definition (`molecular carrier activity` and `has_primary_input` some `protein`) is unchanged.

### Notes on the related term

`GO:0140309 unfolded protein holdase activity` already carries the wording proposed in the issue body (`...escorts it to an acceptor molecule or to a specific location...`). No edit was required there. The `term_tracker_item` for #31601 is already attached to both terms.

### Checklist

- [x] PLAN: Issue analyzed; intent (apply hattrill's revised def, as requested by raymond91125) is unambiguous.
- [x] PRE-VALIDATION: SPARQL QC and ELK reasoning both pass on the unmodified `go-edit.obo`.
- [N/A] RESEARCH: Pure definition-wording change agreed by curators in the thread; no new literature claims introduced. No new PMIDs added (existing `PMID:7628437` retained).
- [x] TERM-SEARCH: Confirmed current state of `GO:0140597`, `GO:0140309`, and parent `GO:0140104` via `obo-grep.pl`.
- [N/A] DESIGN-PATTERNS: No structural/axiomatic change; logical def and parentage untouched.
- [x] EDITS: Performed via `obo-checkout.pl` / edit in `terms/` / `obo-checkin.pl`.
- [N/A] RELATIONSHIPS: Unchanged.
- [N/A] SPECIALIZED-EDITS: Not an obsoletion, chemical, reaction, or taxon-constraint change.
- [x] METADATA: Term is pre-existing; per project rules `created_by`/`creation_date` were not altered. Definition xref `PMID:7628437` retained; `term_tracker_item` for #31601 was already present.
- [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION:
  - `robot verify` against all standard SPARQL QC queries: 0 violations.
  - `robot reason -r ELK`: completed successfully, no unsatisfiable classes.
  - Full `make travis_build` was not run end-to-end (Maven/ODK toolchain not exercised here), but the same logical and SPARQL checks it gates on pass.
- [x] REFERENCE-VALIDATION: No new references introduced.
- [x] CHANGES-COMMITTED: Only `src/ontology/go-edit.obo` modified and committed.
